reviews.review_msg: Worst prof worst accent seems like all the reviews are written by her, they are in such a polished English

Lyn was very understanding and non-judgemental of math difficulties I was having. She is so encouraging, and extremely helpful in office hours (I just wish she had more hours!). She made the dullest class in university a little bit brighter.

Very, very solid overview of Calc 1. She covered all nevessary concepts and practice problems, and the course overall had quizzes, seminars and assignments to stay updated with the material. Highly recommend.
Fantastic lecturer, I always understood the concepts she explained
She's a very good teacher who actually cares about students. This was a great experience for calc 1.

She's a very good prof for math. She goes very slowly during lectures which is a good thing and explains different concepts very well. sometimes she's hard to understand but you can always go to office hours or calculus workshop. MATH150 is a hard course with lots of hw and quizzes weekly so make sure to stay on top of those when studying!!

Theorem: Yes!!! Take it w Lyn or forever regret your life choices.
Proof: look at all the reviews :)

Lyn is very caring and wants her students to succeed. I was scared of Math but now considering to switch to engineering because of her. Exam questions match the seminar problems and assignments. If you review them, you should be fine. Definitely recommend!
Best prof ever: seriously.
Solid prof for first year calc, she's very understanding that first years are new to uni structure, and helps you adapt nicely

The class can seem fast paced, so I definitely recommend lots of practice outside of class to really lock in the concepts. Her lectures are decent, sometimes she's hard to understand, but overall pretty good. The exams matched well with the samples and the seminar questions, so overall I can recommend her
The quizzes can be a serious time crunch at first, but you get used to them, and she's pretty decent at explaining
She willingly wrote me a reference letter for my USRA project application - as a former student from her Calc 2 class, I definitely appreciated that
I'm in this class rn and I can confirm it's going great, I'll admit I'm not a math major and this is one of the few math classes I'll ever need to take, but ngl I'm considering a math minor now
It's a shame that she doesn't teach that many classes. I hope the math dept reads these reviews and realizes that we NEED Lyn
You can tell she knows the difficulties of student life, thus she doesn't make this class any harder than it needs to be.
Excellent teacher
Would give more than 5/5 if I could

The night before our final, she sent a message on the class wide Discord reminding us to eat and sleep properly. See? This is the difference: you have here a Prof who actually cares about the well-being of students.
She's got the weird mix of terrible handwriting yet amazing teaching. It's so hard to explain but I love it somehow
She got a standing ovation at the end of the semester when I took her Calc 2 in Spring of 2024

This is your standard Calc 1 class, I do agree that her accent can be hard to understand and her writing is kinda messy, but she's a good prof and explains stuff pretty well imo, she's good overall tbh

She definitely played a role in me taking more math courses later on. This class, though quite standard, definitely proved foundational for my later classes. She was brand new when I took her course (Spring 2024) and learning the ropes at first, but she improved significantly by the end of the course. Sounds like she's doing well now still!
You thought Calc 2 was this massive barrier of a course? Wrong! Lyn makes it accessible, fun, and you come out realizing the unthinkable: you enjoyed the course and want more.

If Lyn has a million fans, then I am one of them. If Lyn has only one fan, then that is me. If Lyn has no fans, then that means I'm no longer on Earth. If the world is against Lyn, then I am against the world.
Took her for Spring 2024, there were 3 midterms and a final. Very fair on the tests, practically like redoing the assignments in a timed setting. I don't know how you can get more fair than that.

Despite supposedly being "harder" than courses like 157, I feel like 150 is run significantly better. First, you have Lyn the goat, then you get the seminars which are incredibly helpful (shout out to the peer mentors and TAs too)! The only bad part is webassign which is frustrating to use, but that's not her fault at all as it's standardized
Her lectures are highly captivating. It is like watching magic unfold: you unlock the true beauty of the subject.
Lyn was confirmation to me that any prof who uses Discord for their course is goated

Anyone who complains just doesn't know how good they have it. She's easily the best Prof I've had in uni so far, and the consistent reviews here seem to agree. What more can you really ask for lol, fair tests and great lectures
So kind and helpful. I'd have her teach every class if she could

Her accent is very hard to understand, her writing is not clear, doesn't provide clear explanation of t
what units the quizzes are on.

During lectures it's near impossible to understand what she is saying due to her accent.
Good teaching, fair tests and quizzes, responsive to questions, you can't really ask for more.
The nicest prof you can have as a first year. Genuinely an amazing experience, she's so supportive and gives good tips not just for calculus, but for general studying. Can't recommend enough
Amazing professor
Lyn is a great prof. tests were very fair and she actually cared about helping students understand.
Lectures are structured very well and easy to follow. She took the time to explain all the hard concepts if you're unfamiliar with them.

Lyn was an amazing professor. She never had any trick questions on exams. she was very approachable and even made a discord server to help students outside of class and office hours. It's obvious she cares for her students success and sadly that's not a common trait found in most uni professors.

Not bad. Her tests were fair, but I just did very poorly on them. One things is that some profs will give you one webassign a week with around 10-20 question on the material you learned, but she gives you 3 separate ones each with around 6-15 questions, which is more work weekly but good for final exam prep and knowledge overall.
Great professor.

Lyn was super great and super approachable during office hours. Calc 2 in general is a hard course but she made things easier to understand. The questions on the quizzes were from her homework and her test questions were fair. As long as you understand the notes and homework you'll do fine.

If you can take Calculus with her, I highly recommend it. Exams are very fair. Quizzes have same questions from assignments/ web assign questions, so also very fair. Stay consistent with practice every week, and you will do well. Lyn is very accessible and will always answer your questions without making you feel dumb. Goat of teaching calculus.
GOATED prof. I might have written a review here before lol. The classes and the discord server were godsend! Also the prof made a math class so fun and easy but yeah embrace the diy motto!

Great professor who cares about her students but it is a really hard course. Exams and quizzes are very fair, but still hard because this is calc 2. Student discord run by prof is very very helpful! Great all around prof!

Working through the offline and online assignmnents consistently helps alot as the weekly quizzes come straight from those! Moreover, the discord server both the unoffical and the unofficial one r helpful  Moreover, try brushing up precalculus + integration + trig + series and then calc 2 will surely be a breeze. Plus the prof is accessible!

Great prof, easy to approach, fair exams. Just make sure to do all the assignments by yourself and you are good to go. Also make use of the resources provided like office hours of prof and calculus workshop plus the discord server!!
Math requires a lot of work in general, but when Lyn is there, she will help you every step of the way
Calc II is a hard course but lyn is nice, teaches well, and has fair exams. Nothing more you could really ask. Calc II is just very hard lol
hard course but she makes it easy enough.
Very fair exams and quizzes, if you put in the effort.

I would say that she does not really know how to teach. Her Lecture notes are made to confuse students rather than helping them, so you have to study on your own. her tests are easier than what she gives in her lec notes, so that's nice.I would recommend taking this course right after CALC 1, since we use a lot of CALC 1 information in this class.
Good prof but the course is calc 2 so its pretty hard

Cant ask much more in a professor, shes very caring, wants to see you succeed, very fair exams and readily available to answer questions. If you have the chance to take her do it, you still have to put the work in cause the course is calc 2, but you will do fine with her.

Lyn is an amazing prof. She's one of the few that genuinely care about whether students succeed and understand the content. Her exams are fair, and she has plenty of office hours. She's new but honestly way better than most profs who have been here much longer. If you have the opportunity to take 152 with Lyn, I'd strongly recommend it.

She's about everything you would want in a Calc prof! Calc II is known to be tough, but Lyn makes it very manageable. Her lectures are clear, with examples that cover everything you need to know for the assignments. Exams are fair. She is also super approachable in office hours.
Great prof!

Lyn is one of the best professors I've had at SFU. Her lectures are very easy to follow and you can learn pretty much everything if you follow along with what she is teaching in the class. She has office hours that are very accessible and is also willing to do zoom meetings for extra help. She is super caring and genuinely wants you to succeed.

She is a very soft spoken person, so it's sometimes hard to hear her speak during lectures. Regardless, she is very good at simplifying complicated ideas down into Layman's terms, posts organized notes, and is approachable outside of class. Exams and assignments were very fair. Don't be lazy - put in the work outside of class and you'll do fine.

She is new to teaching but is already very good. Her lectures are pretty good; however, she sometimes skips steps but is willing to show them when asked. She consistently ran up to 5 min late every lecture. However, she had a ton of office hours for the final: 6 days and at least 10 hours total. She excels at explaining 1 on 1 in office hours.

Lyn improved drastically during the semester. I believe it was one of her first times teaching, so her lectures were a bit hard to follow at the beginning. This got much better throughout the semester, and she really took her time to make sure people understood before she moved on.

At the beginning it was a bit hard to understand her lecture,  but she got so much better half way through the semester. Super caring and really wants you to succeed. Before finals she had soo many office hours and had zoom meeting for help and explained everything so well with no judgment. Definitely recommend taking a class with her!
She's super helpful and nice. Considering that it's her first year teaching, she did a great job. I'd recommend anyone take a class with Lyn!
she's a pretty good prof overall and always gets right to the point when teaching

She's a super nice person and cares about students. She answers questions perfectly if you ask. Very approachable and gives many good suggestions for studying math. Due to personal issues, I had a very hard time at the beginning of the semester. She's very accommodating to my situation and willing to help me catch up. Exams are fair enough.

Lyn is really chill and explains most concepts nicely. She was a bit confusing at the beginning of the term, but has gotten better recently. She's willing to clarify anything that's unclear. Her class is quite organized and the exams are fair.

Honestly, she's not bad, especially since she is new. She does sometimes skip steps, but she's willing to explain them if you ask. Fairly accessible online and in office hours. Exams are very fair and based on the homework.

I understand that she is new at lecturing but I found it very difficult to follow what she was doing as she doesn't explain the steps thoroughly. I'm not a math whiz so take what I say with a grain of salt but I found that she doesn't show all steps and her writing is scribbly so its hard to go back and look at the posted notes and understand.

She's a great person but not the best lecturer unfortunately, really friendly and i'd suggest going up to her and asking if she can allocate office hours for you, because one on one she is actually pretty good. Personally, I wouldn't take her class again but i also understand she's new. Hope she gets better at this.
